title: Retrieving issues | SonarQube Server Documentation
content:
# Retrieving issues

## [Retrieving issues](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/user-guide/issues/retrieving/#retrieving "Retrieving issues")

In the top navigation bar of SonarQube Server, select **Issues**. Alternatively, you can:

- Retrieve the issues of a given project: [Retrieve the project](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/user-guide/viewing-projects/retrieving-projects/ "Retrieve the project") and select **Issues** in the project navigation bar.
- Retrieve issues from an analysis report by selecting the reported number of issues.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![Issues overview page](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/fdb47e32-8f5d-48a5-9f61-5eb6b70ffe56/issues-overview.png?w=1400&h=855&auto=format&fit=crop "Issues overview page")

The **Issues** page opens with:

1. Search filters in the left-side panel

2. Issue results in the right section of the page

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![Issues filters and results](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/7e632951-3772-4f21-aeb5-5791a4e94e43/issues_search-results.png?w=1400&h=801&auto=format&fit=crop "Issues filters and results")

### [Filtering issues](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/user-guide/issues/retrieving/#filtering-issues "Filtering issues")

In the **Filters**, enter your search criteria to filter the list of issues.

- To retrieve only the new issues (issues detected in [new code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/user-guide/clean-as-you-code/about-new-code/ "new code")), select **Issues in new code** at the top of the **Filters** section.
- To retrieve the issues assigned to you, select **My Issues** at the top of the **Filters** section.
- To retrieve unassigned issues, select **All** at the top of the **Filters** section, open the **Assignee** filter, and select **Not assigned**.

The issue cards are listed in the search results in the right section. An issue card contains the following information:

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/4bf71683-ebae-4e4b-90b6-d721c8a9251e/issues-result-mqr.png?w=1102&h=287&auto=format&fit=crop)

1. Project name and the path to the code file.

2. The name of the rule that triggered the issue.

3. Impacted software quality (in MQR mode) or type (in Standard Experience).

4. Status of the issue.

5. Assignee

6. Clean Code attribute

7. Tags

8. Additional information: line number, estimated time effort to fix the issue, the amount of time that has passed since the introduction of the code.

### [Navigating the issues](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/user-guide/issues/retrieving/#navigating-the-issues "Navigating the issues")

To navigate to an issue select the issue card in the search results and press your Right arrow key. The detail view of the issue opens in the right section, and the search results are shown in the left sidebar. To start a new search, press the Left arrow key or navigate one step back in your browser.

## [Copying the URL of an issue](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/user-guide/issues/retrieving/#copying-url "Copying the URL of an issue")

1. Retrieve the issue and open its detail view as described above in **Retrieving issues**.

2. Select the Link icon as illustrated below.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/1f8a5b16-c029-4e25-ac2c-abad5d0e3e92/issues-copy-link.png?w=1399&h=399&auto=format&fit=crop)
